entry.text: The Crown & Cup is quiet before the rush. A royal notice hangs by the shelves. Tonight, the King dines here.

look.say: "Serve ROYAL ALE and a GOLDEN LOAF upon the high table." — still the plan.
A sealed notice: "His Majesty dines tonight. Serve ROYAL ALE and a GOLDEN LOAF upon the high table."
"Serve ROYAL ALE and a GOLDEN LOAF upon the high table." — still the plan.

take.say: You already know what it says. Ale and a golden loaf.
You take the notice. "Serve ROYAL ALE and a GOLDEN LOAF upon the high table."

use.say: Royal ale from the cellar. Golden loaf from the hearth. Set them on the bar.
"Serve ROYAL ALE and a GOLDEN LOAF upon the high table."

look.say: The mantel is bare now. The iron key is in your pocket.
An iron key rests on the stone mantel. It looks like a cellar key.

take.say: You pocket the iron cellar key.

use.say: Nothing else on the mantel.
You pocket the iron cellar key.

look.say: The bar is empty where the tankard sat.
You already filled a tankard with royal ale.
A clean empty tankard on the bar. Perfect for royal ale.

take.say: You already have the tankard — or what it became.
Your hands are full of frothy ale already.

use.say: Already taken.
You take the empty tankard.

look.say: The oven still glows. The golden loaf is done.
A hot stone oven. Your sack of flour would bake well here.
A stone hearth oven, coals glowing. Good for a golden loaf — if you had flour.

use.say: The loaf is already baked.
Nothing to bake. You need flour — try the courtyard stores.
You work the dough into the oven. Minutes later — a golden loaf, crust singing.

flour.say: You work the dough into the oven. Minutes later — a golden loaf, crust singing.

tankard.say: The oven is for bread, not ale.

royal_ale.say: Warm ale is a crime. The bar is the place.

notice.say: The notice already told you: bake a golden loaf.

look.say: Ale and loaf wait for His Majesty. The Crown & Cup is ready.
The feast is set.
Royal ale sits on the bar. Still needs the golden loaf.
The golden loaf sits on the bar. Still needs royal ale.
The serving bar — where the King's feast must be laid: royal ale and a golden loaf.

use.say: Everything is set. The King will be pleased.
You set the frothy ale and the golden loaf upon the bar. Perfect.
use.win: A horn sounds outside. The King arrives to ale and fresh bread. The Crown & Cup is saved — and so is your job. Well done, Gareth!

use.say: You set the royal ale on the bar. Now the golden loaf.
You set the golden loaf on the bar. Now the royal ale.
Loaf joins the ale. The feast is complete!
use.win: A horn sounds outside. The King arrives to ale and fresh bread. The Crown & Cup is saved — and so is your job. Well done, Gareth!

use.say: Ale joins the loaf. The feast is complete!
use.win: A horn sounds outside. The King arrives to ale and fresh bread. The Crown & Cup is saved — and so is your job. Well done, Gareth!

use.say: The bar waits for royal ale and a golden loaf. Check the notice if you've forgotten.

royal_ale.say: Ale joins the loaf. The feast is complete!
royal_ale.win: A horn sounds outside. The King arrives to ale and fresh bread. The Crown & Cup is saved — and so is your job. Well done, Gareth!

royal_ale.say: You set the royal ale on the bar. Now the golden loaf.

loaf.say: Loaf joins the ale. The feast is complete!
loaf.win: A horn sounds outside. The King arrives to ale and fresh bread. The Crown & Cup is saved — and so is your job. Well done, Gareth!

loaf.say: You set the golden loaf on the bar. Now the royal ale.

tankard.say: Fill it with royal ale from the cellar first.

flour.say: Bake it in the oven first.

notice.say: You've read it. Now do what it says.

look.say: The serving bar. Empty tankard gone — ready for the feast.
A solid oak bar. An empty tankard waits. This is where the King will be served.

use.say: Set the feast on the serving bar when you have ale and loaf.

look.say: Frothy royal ale, set for His Majesty.

use.say: It's already served.

look.say: A golden loaf, still warm, waiting on the bar.

take.say: The King should have the first slice.

use.say: It's already served.

look.say: The cellar door stands open. Cool air rises from the stairs.
A locked cellar door. You have the key.
A heavy wooden door to the cellar — shut tight and locked. The key was on the hearth mantel.

use.say: You head down into the cool dark.
Locked. Try the hearth mantel for a key.
The key turns. The cellar door swings open.

cellar_key.say: The key turns. The cellar door swings open.

tankard.say: The door wants a key, not a mug.

notice.say: The notice wants ale from beyond this door.

look.say: Daylight and cobbles. The courtyard stores are that way.

use.say: You step out into the yard.

objectives.text: Goal: set the golden loaf on the bar
Goal: set the royal ale on the bar
Goal: set ale and loaf on the serving bar
Goal: set the feast on the serving bar
Goal: bake the flour in the hearth oven
Goal: get flour from the courtyard, then bake a loaf
Goal: fill the tankard at the crowned cask in the cellar
Goal: fill a tankard with royal ale from the cellar
Goal: bake flour in the oven; fill tankard in the cellar
Goal: bake the flour in the hearth oven
Goal: fill the tankard at the crowned cask below
Goal: take the tankard, fill it at the crowned cask
Goal: get the cellar key from the mantel
Goal: read the royal notice by the shelves
